reasoning: The Jan 25 resolution date falls strictly within the period where the January 2026 FIDE ranking list governs. Because FIDE classical rankings are updated monthly (on the 1st), Wei Yi's ranking on Jan 25 will almost certainly be exactly what it is on Jan 18 (Rank #10). For the Feb 17 date, the February 1 FIDE list applies. Wei Yi is not playing classical chess in January (his Tata Steel India event is Rapid/Blitz, which does not affect classical ratings). While some key competitors (Gukesh #9, Praggnanandhaa #8, Abdusattorov #12) are playing the classical Tata Steel Masters in Wijk aan Zee in January, the tournament ends on February 1. FIDE regulations heavily favor rating events in the month they conclude, meaning Wijk aan Zee will very likely only be published on the March 1 FIDE list, keeping the Feb 1 list untouched for these players. Furthermore, even if Wijk aan Zee is rated in February, Wei Yi (#10, 2754) is insulated: there are multiple players directly above him in the top 10 participating who could lose points and fall below him, and only Abdusattorov (#12, 2751) in the field immediately below him who is close enough to potentially pass him. Accordingly, Wei Yi's expected rank is skewed towards remaining at 10 or improving.
